req.title: System-Admin updates training-course
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a training-course through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for requirement:
An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a training-course
through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit
record must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime, timezone


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def system_admin_user():
    """Return a fully-authenticated system-admin user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = 1
    user.username = "admin_user"
    user.role = "system-admin"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def non_admin_user():
    """Return an authenticated user who is NOT a system-admin."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = 2
    user.username = "regular_user"
    user.role = "learner"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """Return an unauthenticated (anonymous) user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = None
    user.username = None
    user.is_authenticated = False
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def existing_training_course():
    """Return a training course domain object representing persisted data."""
    course = MagicMock()
    course.id = 42
    course.title = "Original Title"
    course.description = "Original description."
    course.duration_hours = 8
    course.category = "Compliance"
    course.is_active = True
    return course


@pytest.fixture
def valid_update_payload():
    """Return a dict that satisfies all mandatory field rules."""
    return {
        "title": "Updated Safety Training",
        "description": "Comprehensive updated safety module.",
        "duration_hours": 4,
        "category": "Health & Safety",
        "is_active": True,
    }


@pytest.fixture
def audit_service():
    """Return a mock audit-log service."""
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def course_repository(existing_training_course):
    """Return a mock repository wired to return *existing_training_course*."""
    repo = MagicMock()
    repo.get_by_id.return_value = existing_training_course
    repo.save.return_value = existing_training_course
    return repo


@pytest.fixture
def update_course_use_case(course_repository, audit_service):
    """
    Instantiate the use-case / service under test with injected dependencies.
    Replace with your real import, e.g.:
        from app.use_cases.training_courses import UpdateTrainingCourseUseCase
    """
    from app.use_cases.training_courses import UpdateTrainingCourseUseCase  # noqa: PLC0415

    return UpdateTrainingCourseUseCase(
        course_repository=course_repository,
        audit_service=audit_service,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def ui_form_validator():
    """
    Instantiate the UI-form validator under test.
    Replace with your real import, e.g.:
        from app.forms.training_courses import TrainingCourseUpdateForm
    """
    from app.forms.training_courses import TrainingCourseUpdateForm  # noqa: PLC0415

    return TrainingCourseUpdateForm


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

MANDATORY_FIELDS = ["title", "description", "duration_hours", "category"]


# ===========================================================================
# 1. Authentication & Authorisation
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuthentication:
    """Only authenticated system-admins may invoke the update flow."""

    def test_authenticated_system_admin_is_permitted(
        self, update_course_use_case, system_admin_user, valid_update_payload
    ):
        """A system-admin user must be allowed to trigger an update."""
        result = update_course_use_case.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            course_id=42,
            payload=valid_update_payload,
        )
        assert result.success is True

    def test_unauthenticated_user_is_rejected(
        self, update_course_use_case, unauthenticated_user, valid_update_payload
    ):
        """An anonymous (unauthenticated) user must not be able to update."""
        from app.exceptions import AuthenticationRequiredError  # noqa: PLC0415

        with pytest.raises(AuthenticationRequiredError):
            update_course_use_case.execute(
                actor=unauthenticated_user,
                course_id=42,
                payload=valid_update_payload,
            )

    def test_non_admin_authenticated_user_is_rejected(
        self, update_course_use_case, non_admin_user, valid_update_payload
    ):
        """A learner or other non-admin role must receive a permission error."""
        from app.exceptions import PermissionDeniedError  # noqa: PLC0415

        with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError):
            update_course_use_case.execute(
                actor=non_admin_user,
                course_id=42,
                payload=valid_update_payload,
            )


# ===========================================================================
# 2. Mandatory Field Validation – UI Form Layer
# ===========================================================================

class TestMandatoryFieldValidation:
    """
    Each mandatory field, when missing or blank, must cause form validation
    to fail with a clear field-level error.
    """

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_missing_mandatory_field_fails_validation(
        self, ui_form_validator, valid_update_payload, missing_field
    ):
        """Form must be invalid when any single mandatory field is absent."""
        payload = {k: v for k, v in valid_update_payload.items() if k != missing_field}
        form = ui_form_validator(data=payload)

        assert form.is_valid() is False
        assert missing_field in form.errors, (
            f"Expected validation error for missing field '{missing_field}'"
        )

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("blank_field", ["title", "description", "category"])
    def test_blank_string_mandatory_field_fails_validation(
        self, ui_form_validator, valid_update_payload, blank_field
    ):
        """String mandatory fields must not accept empty or whitespace-only values."""
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, blank_field: "   "}
        form = ui_form_validator(data=payload)

        assert form.is_valid() is False
        assert blank_field in form.errors

    def test_zero_duration_fails_validation(
        self, ui_form_validator, valid_update_payload
    ):
        """duration_hours must be a positive integer (> 0)."""
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "duration_hours": 0}
        form = ui_form_validator(data=payload)

        assert form.is_valid() is False
        assert "duration_hours" in form.errors

    def test_negative_duration_fails_validation(
        self, ui_form_validator, valid_update_payload
    ):
        """Negative values for duration_hours must be rejected."""
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "duration_hours": -5}
        form = ui_form_validator(data=payload)

        assert form.is_valid() is False
        assert "duration_hours" in form.errors

    def test_non_numeric_duration_fails_validation(
        self, ui_form_validator, valid_update_payload
    ):
        """A non-numeric string for duration_hours must fail validation."""
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "duration_hours": "abc"}
        form = ui_form_validator(data=payload)

        assert form.is_valid() is False
        assert "duration_hours" in form.errors

    def test_all_mandatory_fields_present_passes_validation(
        self, ui_form_validator, valid_update_payload
    ):
        """A fully-populated payload must pass form validation."""
        form = ui_form_validator(data=valid_update_payload)
        assert form.is_valid() is True, f"Unexpected form errors: {form.errors}"

    def test_optional_fields_absence_does_not_block_validation(
        self, ui_form_validator, valid_update_payload
    ):
        """Removing optional fields must not cause validation to fail."""
        # is_active is optional; strip it and confirm form still valid
        payload = {k: v for k, v in valid_update_payload.items() if k != "is_active"}
        form = ui_form_validator(data=payload)
        assert form.is_valid() is True, f"Unexpected form errors: {form.errors}"

    def test_title_exceeds_max_length_fails_validation(
        self, ui_form_validator, valid_update_payload
    ):
        """Titles that exceed the maximum allowed length must be rejected."""
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "title": "A" * 256}
        form = ui_form_validator(data=payload)

        assert form.is_valid() is False
        assert "title" in form.errors


# ===========================================================================
# 3. Successful Update Flow
# ===========================================================================

class TestSuccessfulUpdate:
    """When all preconditions are met the course must be persisted correctly."""

    def test_use_case_returns_success_result(
        self,
        update_course_use_case,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_update_payload,
    ):
        """execute() must return a result object with success=True."""
        result = update_course_use_case.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            course_id=42,
            payload=valid_update_payload,
        )
        assert result.success is True

    def test_repository_save_is_called_once(
        self,
        update_course_use_case,
        course_repository,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_update_payload,
    ):
        """The repository's save method must be called exactly once."""
        update_course_use_case.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            course_id=42,
            payload=valid_update_payload,
        )
        course_repository.save.assert_called_once()

    def test_repository_saves_correct_course_id(
        self,
        update_course_use_case,
        course_repository,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_update_payload,
    ):
        """The saved entity must carry the same course ID as requested."""
        update_course_use_case.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            course_id=42,
            payload=valid_update_payload,
        )
        saved_entity = course_repository.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert saved_entity.id == 42

    def test_updated_fields_are_persisted(
        self,
        update_course_use_case,
        course_repository,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_update_payload,
    ):
        """All fields supplied in the payload must be reflected on the saved entity."""
        update_course_use_case.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            course_id=42,
            payload=valid_update_payload,
        )
        saved_entity = course_repository.save.call_args[0][0]

        assert saved_entity.title == valid_update_payload["title"]
        assert saved_entity.description == valid_update_payload["description"]
        assert saved_entity.duration_hours == valid_update_payload["duration_hours"]
        assert saved_entity.category == valid_update_payload["category"]

    def test_result_contains_updated_course(
        self,
        update_course_use_case,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_update_payload,
    ):
        """The result object must expose the updated course data."""
        result = update_course_use_case.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            course_id=42,
            payload=valid_update_payload,
        )
        assert result.course is not None
        assert result.course.id == 42


# ===========================================================================
# 4. Audit Record
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuditRecord:
    """An audit record must be written on every successful update."""

    def test_audit_service_called_on_success(
        self,
        update_course_use_case,
        audit_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_update_payload,
    ):
        """audit_service.log() must be invoked exactly once after a successful update."""
        update_course_use_case.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            course_id=42,
            payload=valid_update_payload,
        )
        audit_service.log.assert_called_once()

    def test_audit_record_contains_actor_id(
        self,
        update_course_use_case,
        audit_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_update_payload,
    ):
        """The audit record must identify the user who performed the update."""
        update_course_use_case.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            course_id=42,
            payload=valid_update_payload,
        )
        _, kwargs = audit_service.log.call_args
        assert kwargs.get("actor_id") == system_admin_user.id, (
            "Audit record must store the actor's user ID"
        )

    def test_audit_record_contains_course_id(
        self,
        update_course_use_case,
        audit_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_update_payload,
    ):
        """The audit record must reference the training-course that was updated."""
        update_course_use_case.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            course_id=42,
            payload=valid_update_payload,
        )
        _, kwargs = audit_service.log.call_args
        assert kwargs.get("resource_id") == 42

    def test_audit_record_action_is_update(
        self,
        update_course_use_case,
        audit_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_update_payload,
    ):
        """The audit record's action field must clearly indicate an update operation."""
        update_course_use_case.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            course_id=42,
            payload=valid_update_payload,
        )
        _, kwargs = audit_service.log.call_args
        assert kwargs.get("action") == "UPDATE_TRAINING_COURSE"

    def test_audit_record_contains_timestamp(
        self,
        update_course_use_case,
        audit_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_update_payload,
    ):
        """The audit record must include a UTC timestamp."""
        before = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

        update_course_use_case.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            course_id=42,
            payload=valid_update_payload,
        )

        after = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        _, kwargs = audit_service.log.call_args
        timestamp = kwargs.get("timestamp")

        assert timestamp is not None, "Audit record must include a timestamp"
        assert before <= timestamp <= after, (
            "Audit timestamp must fall within the execution window"
        )

    def test_audit_record_contains_changed_fields(
        self,
        update_course_use_case,
        audit_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_update_payload,
    ):
        """The audit record must capture which fields were changed."""
        update_course_use_case.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            course_id=42,
            payload=valid_update_payload,
        )
        _, kwargs = audit_service.log.call_args
        changed_fields = kwargs.get("changed_fields")

        assert changed_fields is not None, "Audit record must list changed fields"
        assert isinstance(changed_fields, dict)

    def test_audit_service_not_called_on_validation_failure(
        self,
        update_course_use_case,
        audit_service,
        system_admin_user,
    ):
        """No audit record must be written when the update payload is invalid."""
        from app.exceptions import ValidationError  # noqa: PLC0415

        invalid_payload = {"title": ""}  # missing mandatory fields

        with pytest.raises((ValidationError, Exception)):
            update_course_use_case.execute(
                actor=system_admin_user,
                course_id=42,
                payload=invalid_payload,
            )

        audit_service.log.assert_not_called()

    def test_audit_service_not_called_on_permission_failure(
        self,
        update_course_use_case,
        audit_service,
        non_admin_user,
        valid_update_payload,
    ):
        """No audit record must be written when the actor lacks permission."""
        from app.exceptions import PermissionDeniedError  # noqa: PLC0415

        with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError):
            update_course_use_case.execute(
                actor=non_admin_user,
                course_id=42,
                payload=valid_update_payload,
            )

        audit_service.log.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 5. Edge Cases & Error Handling
# ===========================================================================

class TestEdgeCases:
    """Boundary conditions and error scenarios."""

    def test_updating_non_existent_course_raises_error(
        self,
        update_course_use_case,
        course_repository,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_update_payload,
    ):
        """Attempting to update a course that does not exist must raise an error."""
        from app.exceptions import CourseNotFoundError  # noqa: PLC0415

        course_repository.get_by_id.return_value = None

        with pytest.raises(CourseNotFoundError):
            update_course_use_case.execute(
                actor=system_admin_user,
                course_id=9999,
                payload=valid_update_payload,
            )

    def test_repository_failure_propagates_and_no_audit_written(
        self,
        update_course_use_case,
        course_repository,
        audit_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_update_payload,
    ):
        """If the repository raises an error the audit record must NOT be written."""
        course_repository.save.side_effect = RuntimeError("DB connection lost")

        with pytest.raises(RuntimeError):
            update_course_use_case.execute(
                actor=system_admin_user,
                course_id=42,
                payload=valid_update_payload,
            )

        audit_service.log.assert_not_called()

    def test_title_at_maximum_allowed_length_passes_validation(
        self, ui_form_validator, valid_update_payload
    ):
        """A title exactly at the maximum length boundary must be accepted."""
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "title": "A" * 255}
        form = ui_form_validator(data=payload)
        assert form.is_valid() is True, f"Unexpected form errors: {form.errors}"

    def test_duration_of_one_hour_is_valid(
        self, ui_form_validator, valid_update_payload
    ):
        """The minimum positive duration (1 hour) must pass validation."""
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "duration_hours": 1}
        form = ui_form_validator(data=payload)
        assert form.is_valid() is True, f"Unexpected form errors: {form.errors}"

    def test_concurrent_update_uses_latest_persisted_state(
        self,
        update_course_use_case,
        course_repository,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_update_payload,
    ):
        """
        The use-case must fetch the current state from the repository before
        applying changes (optimistic-lock / read-before-write pattern).
        """
        update_course_use_case.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            course_id=42,
            payload=valid_update_payload,
        )
        course_repository.get_by_id.assert_called_once_with(42)

    def test_idempotent_update_with_same_payload_writes_audit(
        self,
        update_course_use_case,
        audit_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_update_payload,
    ):
        """
        Submitting the same data twice must still record an audit entry each
        time (the system cannot silently swallow repeated admin actions).
        """
        update_course_use_case.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            course_id=42,
            payload=valid_update_payload,
        )
        update_course_use_case.execute(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            course_id=42,
            payload=valid_update_payload,
        )
        assert audit_service.log.call_count == 2

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("xss_value", [
        "<script>alert('xss')</script>",
        "'; DROP TABLE courses; --",
        "<img src=x onerror=alert(1)>",
    ])
    def test_malicious_input_in_title_fails_or_is_sanitised(
        self, ui_form_validator, valid_update_payload, xss_value
    ):
        """
        Potentially harmful strings must either fail validation or be sanitised
        before reaching the persistence layer.
        """
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "title": xss_value}
        form = ui_form_validator(data=payload)

        if form.is_valid():
            cleaned = form.cleaned_data["title"]
            assert "<script>" not in cleaned
            assert "DROP TABLE" not in cleaned
            assert "onerror" not in cleaned
        else:
            assert "title" in form.errors
```

---

## Key design decisions

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| **Layering** | Tests are split across the **UI form validator** (field rules) and the **use-case** (orchestration, auth, audit) so each layer can fail independently. |
| **Fixtures** | Shared fixtures (`system_admin_user`, `valid_update_payload`, `course_repository`, `audit_service`) keep individual tests focused and DRY. |
| **Parametrisation** | `@pytest.mark.parametrize` covers every mandatory field for missing/blank checks and common XSS/injection strings. |
| **Audit assertions** | Every attribute of the audit record (actor, resource, action, timestamp, changed fields) is verified in isolation so a single-field regression is pinpointed immediately. |
| **Negative audit tests** | Explicit tests confirm the audit service is **not** called on auth failure, permission failure, validation failure, or repository error. |
| **Imports inside tests** | Real application classes are imported inside test bodies so the file remains syntax-valid even before the production code exists, and import errors surface as clear test failures. |

MANDATORY_FIELDS = ["title", "description", "duration_hours", "category"]


# ===========================================================================

class TestAuthentication:
